respectful.definition: Showing that you care about someone's feelings and treat them well.
respectful.example: She always speaks about her ex in a respectful way.

appreciate.definition: To see the good in someone or something and be thankful.
appreciate.example: I appreciate your help with the children.

emphasizing.definition: Giving special attention to something; stressing it.
emphasizing.example: She kept emphasizing his faults instead of his good points.

faults.definition: Mistakes or bad qualities in a person.
faults.example: Everyone has faults, but we also have good qualities.

impossible.definition: Not able to happen or be done.
impossible.example: It seemed impossible to stay calm during the argument.

internalized.definition: Made something part of your own thinking or beliefs.
internalized.example: The child internalized the message that he was not good enough.

torn.definition: Pulled in two different directions; feeling split.
torn.example: She felt torn between her two parents.

loyal.example: Children feel loyal to both their parents.

negotiate.definition: To discuss something in order to reach an agreement.
negotiate.example: The parents tried to negotiate a fair schedule.

split.definition: A separation or break, especially in a relationship.
split.example: After the split, they shared care of the children.

fares.definition: Does in a situation; gets along.
fares.example: A child fares better when both parents cooperate.

cooperate.example: The parents learned to cooperate for their child's sake.

communicate.definition: To share information or feelings with someone.
communicate.example: It is important to communicate clearly with your children.

aggressive.definition: Acting in an angry or violent way.
aggressive.example: The child became aggressive when he felt unheard.

resident.example: The non-resident parent visits on weekends.

separation.definition: When two people stop living together.
separation.example: After the separation, the father moved to another city.

self-esteem.definition: How much you like and value yourself.
self-esteem.example: A child with low self-esteem may feel they are not good enough.

committed.definition: Dedicated to something or someone for a long time.
committed.example: They were not a committed couple but enjoyed each other.

hugely.definition: Very much; to a great degree.
hugely.example: They enjoyed each other's company hugely.

extreme.definition: Very serious or unusual; far beyond normal.
extreme.example: Her story may sound extreme, but it is not uncommon.

disagreements.definition: Situations where people do not agree with each other.
disagreements.example: All parents have disagreements about how to raise children.

pregnant.definition: Having a baby growing inside you.
pregnant.example: When she got pregnant, their lives changed.

abortion.definition: A medical procedure to end a pregnancy.
abortion.example: He assumed she would have an abortion.

furious.example: He was furious when he heard the news.

sever.definition: To cut off or end completely.
sever.example: He tried to sever their connection after the breakup.

minimal.example: He pays minimal maintenance for his child.

maintenance.definition: Money paid regularly to support a child or former partner.
maintenance.example: She receives monthly maintenance for her son.

humiliating.definition: Making someone feel very embarrassed or ashamed.
humiliating.example: The humiliating process made her feel terrible.

paternity.definition: The fact of being someone's father.
paternity.example: A paternity test proved he was the father.

threatened.definition: Feeling scared that something bad will happen.
threatened.example: He felt threatened by the idea of being a father.

acknowledge.definition: To accept or admit that something is true.
acknowledge.example: It is important to acknowledge your child's feelings.

dependant.definition: A person who relies on someone else for money or care.
dependant.example: A child is a dependant who needs your support.

albeit.example: He is a kind person, albeit a quiet one.

dependent.definition: Needing someone else for help or support.
dependent.example: Young children are dependent on their parents.

catalyst.definition: Something that causes a big change.
catalyst.example: Having a baby was a catalyst for change in their lives.

enrichment.definition: Something that makes your life richer and more meaningful.
enrichment.example: A child is a source of enrichment in your life.

cease.example: A child does not cease to exist just because they are ignored.

instinctively.definition: Done naturally, without thinking about it.
instinctively.example: She instinctively knew what her child needed.

desertion.definition: The act of leaving someone when you should stay.
desertion.example: The child may take his father's desertion personally.

distort.definition: To change the shape or meaning of something in a wrong way.
distort.example: His father's absence might distort how he sees himself.

regards.definition: Thinks about or sees something in a certain way.
regards.example: How he regards his gender may be affected.

gender.definition: Being male, female, or another identity.
gender.example: The child may have questions about gender as he grows up.

pitfalls.example: Being aware of the pitfalls can help you avoid them.

guarantee.definition: A promise that something will happen for sure.
guarantee.example: There is no guarantee everything will be perfect.

prescription.definition: A set of instructions or a solution for fixing something.
prescription.example: There is no prescription to make everything okay.

involved.definition: Taking an active part in something.
involved.example: She has a lot of loving and involved family members.

conjure.definition: To create something as if by magic.
conjure.example: It is not easy to conjure up a perfect relationship.

cooperative.example: A cooperative relationship between parents helps the child.

lacking.example: When cooperation is lacking, children suffer.
